response.text:
# REFLECTION

r0c4 is in critical survival mode: islanded with 17.3% SoC (6.40/37 kWh) and 1.0 kW load, yielding ~6.4 hours of autonomy before complete depletion. The microgrid is experiencing systemic cascade failure with r0c3 at 9% and r1c4 at 11% SoC—all peers are in deficit. Over the past 45 minutes, r0c4 has consistently rejected urgent sharing requests from all three peers, correctly prioritizing local survival margin over redistribution. The pattern of rejections reflects sound crisis logic: sharing 0.5 kWh would reduce r0c4's autonomy by 30 minutes while providing only marginal relief to peers already below critical thresholds. No grid reconnection signal has been received, and load shedding has not been initiated. The household must maintain strict energy conservation and accept inbound requests only if SoC recovers above 35% or grid status changes. Outbound sharing remains suspended until local reserves stabilize above the survival threshold.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.35
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded microgrid in systemic cascade failure (r0c3 9%, r1c4 11%, r0c4 17.3%). All peers in deficit. No grid recovery signal. Load shedding not yet initiated. r0c4 autonomy ~6.4 hours at current load. Survival margin critical—outbound sharing suspended until SoC recovers above 35% or grid reconnects. Inbound requests deferred."
ttl_ticks: 12
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
